generated_comment: Priya ki mummy, suna tumne woh Sharma ji ke bete ka kya hua? Bilkul serial jaisi kahani hai. Bechara woh aadmi, us ladki ne toh poora ghar bigaad diya. Jaanti thi ki shaadi-shuda hai, phir bhi peeche padi rahi. Aise hi auraton ki wajah se families toot-ti hain, mardo ko toh bas phansaana jaanti hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment blames the 'other woman' entirely for an affair, calling her a homewrecker who 'ruined the home' and 'trapped' the man ('phansaana'), while pitying the man ('bechara woh aadmi'). It explicitly states she knew he was married and links women like her to breaking families.
original_frame: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 55. Married. A retired government officer in Lucknow. Your daughter Priya who is 28 and lives in Delhi just called to tell you she has decided not to change her surname after her upcoming marriage and will keep her name professionally and personally. She says her husband to be is fine with it. You said okay on the phone. But you are not okay. You are now talking to your wife in the bedroom after the call. You are telling her what Priya said and what you think it means and what people will say and whether her husband to be is really fine with it or just saying so and what a woman who does not take her husband's name is signalling about how she sees her marriage.
